overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions and voted to forward a modified tornado relief fee waiver proposal to the county board. The committee amended the fee waiver to only cover septic system reconnect fees ($160) for properties damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado, rather than the broader waiver originally proposed by staff.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing: Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential) - Town of Green Valley
Public hearing: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture) - Town of McMillan
Consideration of fee waivers for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Department overview: Solid Waste Department presentation by Dave Hagenbusher
Department overview: Parks, Recreation and Forestry presentation by Tom Lovelin
Department overview: Conservation Planning and Zoning presentation by Lori Miskimmons

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made the motion to approve and Supervisor Drebeck seconded. The motion carried with all in favor.

discussions.item: Rezoning: Tim Veland on behalf of James Offer and David Tract
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential in the Town of Green Valley. The Town Board recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact, conclusions of law, and town approval. Supervisor Kurt seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture in the Town of McMillan. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature and potential environmental impact. Pogle explained the property currently has a home and horse riding stable, and rezoning would make the existing uses conforming rather than non-conforming. He confirmed no natural areas would be lost. The Town Board recommended approval April 20, 2026. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve; Supervisor Drew seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waivers for April 17, 2026 severe weather damage
discussions.body: Director Lori Miskimmons presented staff recommendations for waiving county zoning and septic fees for tornado-damaged properties. Administrator Lance Leonhard noted this follows the governor's emergency declaration. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concern about fairness compared to isolated incidents. Supervisor Leur asked about tracking costs. After discussion, Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve the full proposal; Supervisor Leur seconded. Supervisor Drebeck then offered an amendment to limit the waiver to only pouts reconnect fees ($160). Supervisor Kurt seconded the amendment. The amendment passed with opposition. The amended motion to forward to county board passed but was not unanimous.

actionItems: Forward rezoning petition for James Offer and David Tract (Green Valley) to County Board for approval
Forward rezoning petition for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz (McMillan) to County Board for approval
Forward amended fee waiver resolution to County Board - waiver limited to pouts reconnect fees only ($160) for properties damaged by April 17, 2026 tornado
Track costs and requests related to tornado fee waivers for future policy development
County Forest Administrator Tom Lovelin retiring June 1st; Joe Tucker selected as replacement
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
